subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – A Tiger in the Zoo, The Tiger, The Panther
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the themes of captivity, freedom, and human intervention in the poems
Interpret symbols and imagery to understand tigers’ emotions
Develop inferential comprehension and critical thinking skills
Compare and contrast the poets’ perspectives
Express ideas through discussion, writing, and creative representation

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Poets and Context
topics_covered: Authors: Leslie Norris, Peter Niblett, Rainer Maria Rilke
Historical and environmental context of the poems
Themes of wildness, captivity, and human impact
teaching_strategy: Introduce poets and their background briefly
Ask students to think about wildlife in zoos vs in the wild
student_activities: Predict what each poem might explore based on titles
Discuss experiences or observations related to animals in captivity
assessment: Oral Q&A on poets and context

lesson_plan.title: Comprehension and Analysis – A Tiger in the Zoo
topics_covered: Identify imagery: cage, jungle, stars, terrorising deer
Analyze the poet’s portrayal of the tiger’s emotions
teaching_strategy: Read aloud and explain metaphors and vivid imagery
Highlight the tiger’s inner conflict and frustration
student_activities: Underline words and phrases showing captivity
Discuss the impact of confinement on the tiger’s behavior
assessment: Ask inferential questions: Why does the tiger’s rage remain even in the cage?
Check students’ written analysis of key lines

lesson_plan.title: Comprehension and Analysis – The Tiger & The Panther
topics_covered: Understand symbolism: bars, claws, pupils, ritual dance
Compare depiction of tigers’ confinement in Niblett and Rilke
teaching_strategy: Explain symbolism and metaphor in simple language
Encourage students to infer emotions and mental state of the tiger
student_activities: Highlight words showing tigers’ suppressed instincts
Summarize each poem with focus on tiger’s feelings
assessment: Oral discussion on tiger’s perspective and poet’s intention
Check written summaries for analytical depth

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Comparison
topics_covered: Identify and discuss themes: captivity, freedom, human interference
Compare tigers’ portrayal in all three poems
Analyze poet’s message about nature and human influence
teaching_strategy: Create a Venn diagram or chart comparing poems
Encourage students to support observations with lines from poems
student_activities: Fill chart showing tiger in cage vs wild
Discuss how human presence is reflected in the poems
assessment: Oral Q&A on themes and symbolism

lesson_plan.title: Creative Reflection and Application
topics_covered: Understanding emotional depth of animals in captivity
teaching_strategy: Encourage personal reflection and critical thinking
Summarize key points and poetic devices used
student_activities: Write a short paragraph imagining life of a tiger in captivity vs wild
Draw a visual representation of tiger’s emotions or environment
assessment: Check drawings for symbolism and understanding

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of all three poems
Images of tigers in wild and in cages
Charts for symbolism, imagery, and themes
Flashcards with key words and literary devices
teacher_tip: Encourage students to think beyond literal meaning. Focus on symbolism, emotions, and human impact on nature. Use comparison activities to deepen understanding.
